title: Switch to public bus for daily commute
description: Replace your car commute with the city bus. A single car trip of 10 km emits ~1.9 kg CO₂e; a bus covers the same distance at ~0.89 kg split across many passengers.
unit: per month (20 working days, 10 km each way)

title: Use metro/suburban rail instead of car
description: Indian metro rail emits ~0.041 kg CO₂e per km vs 0.192 kg for a petrol car. Switching a 15 km commute daily saves significant emissions.
unit: per month (20 days, 15 km each way)

title: Work from home 2 days per week
description: Eliminating commute trips 2 days per week cuts transport emissions by 40% for those days. No fuel, no vehicle wear.
unit: per month (8 WFH days, 15 km commute)

title: Carpool with 2+ colleagues
description: Sharing a petrol car with 2 others cuts your personal share of emissions by 67%. Carpooling apps like Quick Ride and BlaBlaCar are popular in Indian metros.
unit: per month (20 days, 20 km commute)

title: Switch from petrol bike to cycling for short trips
description: Replacing motorcycle trips under 5 km with a bicycle eliminates those emissions entirely. Urban cycling is gaining ground with dedicated lanes in Pune, Bengaluru, and Delhi.
unit: per month (2 trips/day, 4 km each)

title: Avoid one domestic flight per month
description: A single DEL–BOM round trip (~2,400 km) emits ~612 kg CO₂e. Replacing it with train travel (Rajdhani: 0.041 kg/km) saves over 500 kg per trip.
unit: per avoided flight (DEL–BOM round trip)

title: Switch to electric two-wheeler
description: An electric scooter (Ola S1, Ather 450X) on India's grid emits ~0.053 kg CO₂e/km vs 0.114 kg for a petrol bike — a 53% reduction per km.
unit: per month (300 km/month)

title: Consolidate errands into one trip per week
description: Batching grocery shopping, bill payments, and errands into a single car trip instead of 4 separate trips cuts vehicle emissions by 75% for those journeys.
unit: per month (3 saved trips, 10 km each)

title: Use autorickshaw/shared cab instead of personal car
description: Autorickshaws emit 0.096 kg CO₂e/km vs 0.192 kg for a petrol car. Using app-based shared cabs halves your transport footprint on those routes.
unit: per month (150 km of replaced car travel)

title: Maintain correct tyre pressure monthly
description: Under-inflated tyres increase fuel consumption by up to 3%. Checking and correcting pressure monthly costs nothing and improves fuel efficiency immediately.
unit: per month (500 km driving)

title: Take the overnight train instead of flying
description: Replace a short-haul flight with an overnight sleeper train. Trains emit ~93% fewer CO₂e per passenger-km than domestic flights in India.
unit: per avoided flight (600 km route, e.g., BLR–HYD)

title: Practice eco-driving: smooth acceleration & braking
description: Aggressive driving increases fuel use by 15–30%. Maintaining steady speed, anticipating stops, and avoiding harsh braking reduces emissions on every journey.
unit: per month (500 km of improved driving)

title: Replace all bulbs with LED lighting
description: LED bulbs use 75% less electricity than incandescent bulbs and last 25× longer. Replacing 10 bulbs (60W equivalent → 9W LED) saves ~50 kWh/month.
unit: per month (10 bulbs, 5 hrs/day)

title: Set AC thermostat to 24°C instead of 18°C
description: Every 1°C increase in AC set-point saves 6% electricity. Raising from 18°C to 24°C saves ~36% on cooling costs — BEE India recommends 24°C as optimal.
unit: per month (6-hour AC use daily, 1.5-ton unit)

title: Install a rooftop solar panel system
description: A 2 kW rooftop solar system generates ~200–240 kWh/month in most Indian cities, replacing grid electricity (0.708 kg CO₂e/kWh) with solar (0.041 kg CO₂e/kWh).
unit: per month (2 kW system, 5 peak sun hours)

title: Switch to 5-star BEE-rated appliances
description: A 5-star AC uses ~22% less energy than a 3-star model. A 5-star refrigerator saves up to 40% vs older models. Upgrades pay back in 2–4 years through electricity savings.
unit: per month (AC + refrigerator upgrade)

title: Unplug devices in standby mode
description: Standby power ('vampire load') accounts for 10% of household electricity use. Unplugging TVs, chargers, and set-top boxes when idle saves ~15–20 kWh/month.
unit: per month (15 kWh saved)

title: Use solar water heater instead of electric geyser
description: An electric geyser heating 100 litres daily uses ~1.5 kWh. A solar water heater covers 70–80% of this need year-round in Indian climates, saving ~30 kWh/month.
unit: per month (100 L/day, 300 sunny days/yr average)

title: Switch from LPG to induction cooking
description: Induction cooktops are 84% efficient vs 40% for LPG burners. However, since India's grid is coal-heavy, this is most beneficial combined with renewable electricity.
unit: per month (moderate cooking, 50% renewable grid)

title: Use ceiling fan instead of AC when below 28°C
description: A ceiling fan uses 70–75W vs 1,500W for a 1.5-ton AC — 95% less electricity. Using a fan on mild days and saving AC for the hottest periods saves significant energy.
unit: per month (fan 8 hrs, AC 2 hrs vs AC 8 hrs daily)

title: Wash clothes in cold water
description: Heating water for laundry accounts for 90% of a washing machine's energy use. Cold water washes are equally effective for most fabrics and save 0.5 kWh per load.
unit: per month (20 loads/month)

title: Install motion sensor lights in common areas
description: Motion-sensor lights in hallways, bathrooms, and staircases eliminate the ~30% of lighting energy wasted when rooms are empty but lights are left on.
unit: per month (3 rooms, 4 hrs/day saved)

title: Opt for green power tariff from your DISCOM
description: Several Indian DISCOMs now offer green tariffs where your electricity is sourced from renewable projects. Check TPDDL, BESCOM, and MSEDCL for renewable purchase options.
unit: per month (70 kWh average home consumption)

title: Use a pressure cooker for cooking
description: Pressure cookers reduce cooking time by 50–75%, cutting LPG use proportionally. For dal, rice, and legumes — the backbone of Indian meals — this is highly effective.
unit: per month (1 LPG cylinder saved every 3 months)

title: Go meat-free on Mondays (Meatless Monday)
description: Beef produces 27 kg CO₂e per kg; chicken 6.9 kg/kg. Replacing meat with lentils (0.9 kg CO₂e/kg) or vegetables (2.0 kg/kg) one day per week yields meaningful savings.
unit: per month (4 meat-free Mondays, 200g meat/meal avoided)

title: Replace beef with chicken or fish
description: Beef has 4× the carbon footprint of chicken (27 vs 6.9 kg CO₂e/kg). Switching 1 kg of beef per week to chicken saves over 80 kg CO₂e per month.
unit: per month (1 kg beef/week replaced)

title: Eat one fully plant-based meal per day
description: A plant-based meal (dal, sabzi, roti) averages ~0.5 kg CO₂e vs a mixed meal (~1.5 kg CO₂e). One daily plant-based meal saves ~30 kg CO₂e per month.
unit: per month (30 plant-based meals)

title: Buy locally grown seasonal produce
description: Imported and out-of-season produce travels thousands of kilometres. Buying from local mandis or kisan bazaars cuts transport emissions and supports Indian farmers.
unit: per month (10 kg produce, 500 km avg transport saved)

title: Reduce food waste by meal planning
description: ~14% of India's food is wasted before purchase; ~17% at household level. Food in landfills produces methane, a potent greenhouse gas. Planning meals cuts this directly.
unit: per month (30% reduction in household food waste)

title: Compost kitchen scraps instead of binning
description: Organic waste in landfills produces methane (28× stronger than CO₂ over 100 years). Home composting diverts this waste and produces free fertiliser for your garden.
unit: per month (5 kg/week of kitchen waste composted)

title: Reduce dairy consumption by 50%
description: Dairy production emits 3.2 kg CO₂e per kg of product. India is the world's largest dairy producer; cutting dairy by half and replacing with plant milks reduces methane from cattle.
unit: per month (6 kg dairy/month replaced with 3 kg)

title: Grow a kitchen herb garden
description: Growing coriander, mint, curry leaves, and chillies at home eliminates packaging, transport, and refrigeration emissions for these frequently purchased items.
unit: per month (herbs previously bought weekly)

title: Choose loose-leaf tea over tea bags
description: Pyramid tea bags use plastic nylon mesh that microplastics shed into your cup. Loose-leaf tea has minimal packaging and zero plastic. A simple, zero-cost switch.
unit: per month (packaging and plastic reduction)

title: Eat more rice and pulses, less bread
description: Rice emits 2.7 kg CO₂e/kg and pulses only 0.9 kg CO₂e/kg — far below wheat bread at ~1.4 kg/kg once processing and packaging are counted. This is already India's strength.
unit: per month (shifting 2 kg bread/week to dals)

title: Buy in bulk to reduce packaging waste
description: Single-serve packaging generates 3–5× more plastic per gram of food. Buying atta, rice, sugar, and lentils in 5–10 kg sacks dramatically cuts per-unit packaging emissions.
unit: per month (5 staple items bought in bulk)

title: Avoid beef once a week (if you eat it)
description: Beef is the highest-carbon food at 27 kg CO₂e/kg — 4× chicken, 30× pulses. Even a single meal reduction per week makes a measurable impact.
unit: per month (4 beef meals of 150g replaced with chicken)

title: Buy second-hand clothing (thrift shopping)
description: New clothing production emits ~30 kg CO₂e per garment (DEFRA 2023). Second-hand clothes have near-zero production emissions. Platforms: OLX, ThriftedIndia, Vinted.
unit: per month (2 new garments replaced with thrifted)

title: Extend smartphone use by 1 extra year
description: A smartphone's manufacturing accounts for 70–80% of its lifetime carbon footprint (~70 kg CO₂e). Using your phone one extra year cuts annual manufacturing demand by half.
unit: per month (amortised over 1 extra year)

title: Repair clothing instead of replacing
description: Extending a garment's life by 9 months cuts its carbon, water, and waste footprints by 20–30%. A cobbler or tailor fixes most common wear issues for ₹50–200.
unit: per month (2 garments repaired vs replaced)

title: Use cloth bags instead of plastic bags
description: A single-use plastic bag emits ~1.6 kg CO₂e over its lifecycle. A cotton tote reused 50+ times has a per-use footprint 30× lower. Mandatory in many Indian states since 2022.
unit: per month (20 plastic bags avoided)

title: Avoid fast fashion: buy fewer, better quality clothes
description: Fast fashion garments are typically worn 7–10 times before disposal. Quality clothing worn 50+ times dramatically reduces per-wear carbon cost.
unit: per month (1 fewer impulsive fashion purchase)

title: Batch online orders to reduce deliveries
description: Each last-mile delivery emits ~0.5 kg CO₂e. Waiting to consolidate 4 separate orders into 1 saves 3 delivery trips — simple and free.
unit: per month (12 reduced delivery trips)

title: Borrow or rent tools and equipment
description: Power tools used 12–13 times per year can be rented from hardware stores. Shared tools mean fewer units manufactured, directly cutting embodied carbon.
unit: per month (3 tool rentals vs purchases)

title: Switch to refillable personal care products
description: Shampoo bars, soap blocks, and refill pouches for hand wash use 60–90% less plastic packaging than standard bottles. Many Indian brands now offer refill options.
unit: per month (shampoo, soap, hand wash)

title: Sell or donate old electronics instead of discarding
description: E-waste in landfills leaches toxins and wastes the embodied carbon already invested in manufacturing. Old phones, laptops, and gadgets have active resale markets on OLX, Cashify.
unit: per item responsibly disposed (amortised monthly)

title: Choose products with minimal packaging
description: In a supermarket, 70% of packaging is unnecessary. Choosing loose vegetables, unboxed products, and brands using minimal packaging reduces upstream production emissions.
unit: per month (weekly grocery shopping)

title: Buy Indian-manufactured products
description: Products imported from China, Europe, or the US carry significant shipping emissions (~0.012 kg CO₂e/tonne-km by sea). Choosing 'Make in India' products reduces transport footprint.
unit: per month (5 product switches to domestic brands)

title: Talk to 3 friends or family about your footprint
description: Social influence is among the most powerful drivers of behaviour change. Sharing your CarbonZero score and pledges with friends multiplies the impact beyond your own household.
unit: estimated indirect savings (3 people influenced)

title: Write to your local MP/MLA about climate policy
description: Political representation matters. A single letter from a constituent on climate policy is statistically weighted as representing 50–100 unheard constituents.
unit: estimated policy leverage (systemic change)

title: Join or support a local tree-planting initiative
description: A mature tree absorbs ~21 kg CO₂ per year. Volunteering in urban afforestation drives (common in Delhi, Mumbai, Bengaluru) directly sequester carbon from the atmosphere.
unit: per month (5 trees planted per session, quarterly)

title: Share climate content on social media
description: Consistent, credible climate communication on Instagram, LinkedIn, or Twitter/X normalises low-carbon behaviour and can reach hundreds of people in your network.
unit: estimated indirect savings (audience reach)

title: Ask your employer to measure and reduce office emissions
description: Office buildings account for a major share of India's commercial energy use. Requesting a carbon audit, LED upgrade, or WFH policy costs nothing and can move large amounts of emissions.
unit: estimated savings (50-person office, basic measures)

title: Switch to a bank or fund with ESG commitments
description: Banks invest your deposits. Fossil fuel projects are still funded by major Indian PSU banks. ESG-aligned banks and mutual funds channel capital toward clean energy instead.
unit: estimated leverage per ₹1 lakh invested annually

title: Educate your children's school on sustainability
description: Schools shape habits for life. Approaching your school's management committee with a sustainability proposal — waste reduction, energy audit, green curriculum — creates lasting change.
unit: estimated impact (200-student school, basic programme)

title: Participate in a local climate strike or march
description: Public demonstrations signal to policymakers that climate action has a strong voting constituency. Attendance at Fridays for Future or local climate events amplifies systemic pressure.
unit: estimated systemic policy influence

title: Choose climate-conscious brands and vote with your wallet
description: Consumer demand shifts corporate behaviour. Supporting brands with verified net-zero commitments (like Tata Power, Mahindra Electric, and Paper Boat) rewards low-carbon supply chains.
unit: estimated upstream corporate savings

title: Volunteer with a river or beach clean-up drive
description: Plastic pollution in waterways contributes to methane emissions from decomposing organic waste. Clean-up events (in Mumbai, Goa, Chennai) prevent plastic from entering marine ecosystems.
unit: per event (methane and ocean sink preservation)

title: Buy carbon offsets for unavoidable flights
description: When flying is unavoidable, purchase verified carbon offsets through Gold Standard or VERRA-certified Indian projects — e.g., cookstove programmes in rural Jharkhand or solar in Rajasthan.
unit: per offset purchase (1 tonne CO₂e ~₹900–₹1,500)
